<findings>

[observation] [info] Cohesion of 0.5 is moderate; the mix of data-file constants, view-id types, and node-culler logic suggests the zone could eventually split into a types sub-layer and a utilities sub-layer if it grows.
[observation] [info] Zone correctly contains zero upward imports within the web package, fulfilling its role as the foundation layer per the CLAUDE.md web internal zone stack.
[observation] [info] web-viewer imports web-shared 5 times and web-unit imports it once — the consumer count is healthy and confirms the layer is genuinely shared rather than feature-specific.
[relationship] [info] web-shared is imported by both web-viewer (5x) and web-unit (1x) with clean unidirectional flow — zero upward imports confirm the foundation contract is holding under actual consumer load
[anti-pattern] [warning] web-shared exposes a complete barrel (index.ts) but the existing boundary-check.test.ts does not assert that web-viewer's 5 imports enter through index.ts rather than directly into data-files.ts, node-culler.ts, or view-id.ts — direct sub-file imports from consumers would erode the foundation layer's interface contract in the same way that sub-zone barrel bypass erodes crash/index.ts encapsulation
[suggestion] [warning] Add a boundary-check.test.ts assertion that no file in src/shared/ imports from src/viewer/, src/server/, or src/viewer/messaging/. This is the missing symmetrical enforcement for the foundation layer: web-shared's zero-coupling property is documented and observed but not mechanically protected.
[suggestion] [warning] The fix for crash-detector.ts importing ViewId is one line: change '../external.js' to '../shared/view-id.js' (or '../shared/index.js'). Until this is done, ViewId's placement in web-shared provides no practical isolation benefit because the import still traverses the viewer layer's external.ts gateway.

</findings>

<insights>

- Acts as the dependency floor for the web package — nothing in web-shared imports from viewer, server, or messaging layers
- node-culler is the only logic file here; the rest are pure type/constant modules, which keeps the layer stable
- Covered by a co-located unit test, maintaining the pattern of testing shared utilities close to their source
- Zone correctly contains zero upward imports within the web package, fulfilling its role as the foundation layer per the CLAUDE.md web internal zone stack.
- Cohesion of 0.5 is moderate; the mix of data-file constants, view-id types, and node-culler logic suggests the zone could eventually split into a types sub-layer and a utilities sub-layer if it grows.
- web-viewer imports web-shared 5 times and web-unit imports it once — the consumer count is healthy and confirms the layer is genuinely shared rather than feature-specific.
- The zone serves both production consumers (web-viewer, 5 imports) and test consumers (web-unit, 1 import) through the same interface — no test-only surface leaks into production code, confirming the foundation layer is correctly abstracted
- hench-agent-monitor has zero imports from web-shared despite being a leaf viewer zone — it skips the foundation layer entirely and depends only on web-viewer, which means any shared utility it needs is routed through the larger viewer layer rather than the stable foundation
- web-shared is imported by both web-viewer (5x) and web-unit (1x) with clean unidirectional flow — zero upward imports confirm the foundation contract is holding under actual consumer load
- ViewId (defined in shared/view-id.ts) is intentionally in the foundation layer to be importable without viewer-layer dependencies. It is correctly re-exported through external.ts for viewer consumers, but crash-detector.ts uses the re-export path instead of the direct path. This means a symbol placed in the foundation layer specifically to break a dependency cycle is being accessed through the very layer it was meant to bypass — negating the architectural investment of extracting it to shared/.
- web-shared exposes a complete barrel (index.ts) but the existing boundary-check.test.ts does not assert that web-viewer's 5 imports enter through index.ts rather than directly into data-files.ts, node-culler.ts, or view-id.ts — direct sub-file imports from consumers would erode the foundation layer's interface contract in the same way that sub-zone barrel bypass erodes crash/index.ts encapsulation
- web-shared (5 files, cohesion 0.5) is the only foundation-layer zone in the monorepo without a corresponding enforcement test asserting it has zero upward imports. The domain-isolation.test.js guards package-level foundation boundaries; web-boundary-tests governs cross-zone directions; but no test specifically asserts that web-shared files never import from web-viewer, web-server, or messaging zones. The gap is invisible until a new file is added to web-shared with an upward import.
- Add a boundary-check.test.ts assertion that no file in src/shared/ imports from src/viewer/, src/server/, or src/viewer/messaging/. This is the missing symmetrical enforcement for the foundation layer: web-shared's zero-coupling property is documented and observed but not mechanically protected.
- The fix for crash-detector.ts importing ViewId is one line: change '../external.js' to '../shared/view-id.js' (or '../shared/index.js'). Until this is done, ViewId's placement in web-shared provides no practical isolation benefit because the import still traverses the viewer layer's external.ts gateway.
- [call graph] 33 internal calls, 0 outgoing, 0 incoming (cohesion: 1, coupling: 0)

</insights>
